reason: Response B has a more structured, deliberate cadence with shorter paragraphs and a more careful rhetorical build — each paragraph advancing a single idea before moving to the next — which matches Claude Sonnet's tendency toward organized prose. Response A, while strong, uses longer run-on constructions with comma-chained clauses that feel more like a different model imitating Claude's expansive style. The closing line of B ("civilization made small enough to fit in a single open hand") is the kind of precise, image-grounded conclusion Claude tends to land on.
